• Emphasis on soil health management, reduced chemical input, and biodiversity conservation in farming systems.
• It also includes provisions for certification of organic produce and promotion of farmer producer organizations (FPOs).
• The initiative aligns with India's commitment to sustainable development goals and enhancing farmers' income through value-added products.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country, - different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System—objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
Food processing and related industries in India— scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
GS3.source: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare
prelimsPoints: New scheme launched for organic farming by Ministry of Agriculture.
Provides financial aid, technical guidance, and market linkages.
Focus on soil health, reduced chemical input, and biodiversity.
Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana (PKVY) is an existing scheme for organic farming.
mainsPoints: Discuss the imperative for promoting organic farming and sustainable agricultural practices in India, considering environmental and economic aspects.
Analyze the key features of the new government scheme and its potential to address challenges faced by farmers transitioning to organic methods.
Examine the role of certification, market linkages, and FPOs in ensuring the viability and profitability of organic produce.
Suggest policy measures to integrate organic farming into mainstream agricultural policy for long-term food security and ecological balance.
GS3.imageDescription: Farmer inspecting healthy organic crops in a field.
GS3.trivia: Sikkim was declared India's first fully organic state in 2016.

GS3.headline: National Cybercrime Report Highlights Surge in Financial Fraud, Calls for Enhanced Digital Security
GS3.explainer:
• A comprehensive report released by the National Cybercrime Coordination Centre (NCCC) revealed a significant increase in financial fraud and cyber-attacks.
• The report detailed various modus operandi, including phishing, online scams, and ransomware attacks targeting individuals and small businesses.
• It emphasized the urgent need for enhanced digital literacy, robust cybersecurity infrastructure, and stricter legal frameworks.
• Law enforcement agencies are urged to improve inter-state and international cooperation to tackle cross-border cybercrime.
• The government plans to launch a national awareness campaign on cybersecurity best practices for citizens and organizations.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
Security agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: NCCC report shows surge in financial fraud and cyber-attacks.
Highlights phishing, online scams, and ransomware.
Calls for enhanced digital literacy and cybersecurity infrastructure.
National Cybercrime Coordination Centre (NCCC) works under the Ministry of Home Affairs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving landscape of cybercrime in India, with a focus on financial fraud, and its implications for national security and economic stability.
Analyze the key recommendations of the NCCC report and their potential in strengthening India's cybersecurity framework.
Examine the challenges in preventing and prosecuting cybercrimes, including jurisdictional issues and technological complexities.
Suggest a multi-stakeholder approach involving government, private sector, and citizens to build a resilient and secure digital ecosystem.
GS3.imageDescription: A person's hand typing on a laptop with digital lock icons overlaid, symbolizing cybersecurity.
GS3.trivia: India ranks among the top countries targeted by cyber-attacks globally.

GS3.headline: India Upgrades Antarctic Research Station, Bolsters Polar Science Capabilities
GS3.explainer:
• India announced significant upgrades to its Maitri research station in Antarctica, enhancing infrastructure and scientific research capabilities.
• The upgrades include advanced laboratories, improved living quarters, and sustainable energy systems to support year-round operations.
• This move strengthens India's commitment to polar science, climate change research, and international collaboration in the Antarctic region.
• Researchers will focus on glaciology, oceanography, atmospheric sciences, and biodiversity studies.
• India is a signatory to the Antarctic Treaty System, emphasizing peaceful scientific research and environmental protection.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Awareness in the fields of Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: India upgrades Maitri research station in Antarctica.
Aims to enhance polar science and climate change research.
India is a signatory to the Antarctic Treaty System.
Other Indian Antarctic stations include Dakshin Gangotri (decommissioned) and Bharati.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic and scientific importance of India's research stations in Antarctica for global climate change studies and resource exploration.
Analyze the challenges involved in maintaining and operating research facilities in extreme polar environments.
Examine India's role and responsibilities under the Antarctic Treaty System in promoting peaceful scientific research and environmental protection.
Suggest how India can further leverage its polar research capabilities to contribute to international scientific understanding and policy-making.
GS3.imageDescription: India's Maitri research station amidst snow-covered landscape in Antarctica.
GS3.trivia: India launched its first expedition to Antarctica in 1981, establishing its first research station, Dakshin Gangotri, in 1983.
